RoA_A01_CNMC1_VO_S001_001.source: Southshore, one month after the fall of Stormwind Keep.

RoA_A01_CNMC1_VO_S001_002.source: A proper landing site…. Are we in Lordaeron?

RoA_A01_CNMC1_VO_S001_003.source: Indeed. The township of Soutshore, between Dalaran and Alterac.

RoA_A01_CNMC1_VO_S001_004.source: Thank you, Mayor Marcus. I understand you can’t spare much, but we are grateful for any aid.

RoA_A01_CNMC1_VO_S001_005.source: Of course, Lord Lothar. We wouldn’t let women and children starve. But tell me now, why have you and your people fled across the sea? What happened in Azeroth?

RoA_A01_CNMC1_VO_S001_007.source: My lord. I have sent ravens to Dalaran and Lordaeron. Lord Antonidas is already aware of the scale of the threat, and I believe he will convince King Terenas to summon the lords of the remaining kingdoms.

RoA_A01_CNMC1_VO_S001_008.source: We must not delay, Khadgar. I must speak with the king at once. We dare not give the Horde time to move again.

RoA_A01_CNMC1_VO_S001_009.source: Lordaeron City is no short distance! I will send someone ahead on a fast horse to warn them of your approach.

RoA_A01_CNMC1_VO_S001_010.source: And as for your people here, we will take care of them until you return.

RoA_A01_CNMC1_VO_S001_013.source: You will take charge of settling our people in this region. Make sure everything is in order until we return.

RoA_A01_CNMC1_VO_S001_014.source: I will do my best, Lord Lothar.

RoA_A01_CNMC1_VO_S002_001.source: One week later — Capital City of Lordaeron.

RoA_A01_CNMC1_VO_S002_002.source: Your Grace, the survivors of Stormwind.

RoA_A01_CNMC1_VO_S002_003.source: Your Majesty. As lord of this land, I offer my hospitality and protection to you. The heir of Azeroth deserves no less.

RoA_A01_CNMC1_VO_S002_005.source: You must know, I grieved when I learned the news of your noble father’s death. King Llane was a good man and we counted him as a friend and an ally. Know that we shall do all in our power to restore you to your throne.

RoA_A01_CNMC1_VO_S002_006.source: You bear the royal sigil of Azeroth, yet I do not know who you are. Are you the prince’s protector?

RoA_A01_CNMC1_VO_S002_007.source: Your Majesty, I am Anduin Lothar, Knight-Champion of Azeroth and a close friend to the late King Llane. And these are my companions…

RoA_A01_CNMC1_VO_S002_008.source: …we come before you to discuss a matter of grave and urgent importance!

RoA_A01_CNMC1_VO_S002_009.source: Ah, Lord Lothar… you are of a proud and ancient bloodline, and your reputation precedes you. You shall have your audience.

RoA_A01_CNMC1_VO_S002_010.source: You must be tired, your Majesty. Let’s take rest in the palace.

RoA_A01_CNMC1_VO_S003_001.source: Thank you all for coming, I know the request seemed sudden, but we have matters of grave importance to discuss and time seems to be of the essence.

RoA_A01_CNMC1_VO_S003_002.source: Such a haste for a gathering unseen in decades. You better have important reasons indeed, I missed a royal hunt.

RoA_A01_CNMC1_VO_S003_003.source: I present to you Anduin Lothar, Champion of Stormwind. He has come here as a messenger. I think it best if I let him tell you himself what he has seen and what we may soon expect ourselves.

RoA_A01_CNMC1_VO_S003_004.source: Your Majesties, I thank you for attending this meeting, and for listening to what I have to say. I am no poet or diplomat but a warrior, so I will keep my words brief and blunt. I must tell you that my home, Stormwind, is no more.

RoA_A01_CNMC1_VO_S003_005.source: My lords! Lord Lothar, please continue. I have been to Stormwind before, and I admired its grandness and strength. What could destroy such a city?

RoA_A01_CNMC1_VO_S003_006.source: It fell before a Horde of creatures known as orcs.

RoA_A01_CNMC1_VO_S003_007.source: Orcs? Are they some kind of trolls? And where did this horde come from? As far as I know, you have dealt with the trolls of the southern jungles before.

RoA_A01_CNMC1_VO_S003_008.source: No trolls, Your Grace. They are an alien race, who have invaded our lands through a magical rift hidden somewhere within the southern fringes of the Black Morass.

RoA_A01_CNMC1_VO_S003_009.source: They are terrible foes, as tall as a man and far stronger, with bestial features, green skin, and red eyes.

RoA_A01_CNMC1_VO_S003_010.source: Know this — the Horde will not stop at Stormwind. They mean to claim the entire world, and they have the might and the numbers to do so. Nor do they lack magical powers.

RoA_A01_CNMC1_VO_S003_011.source: Once they have finished with my homeland, they will find a way to cross the ocean and come for the rest of you.

RoA_A01_CNMC1_VO_S003_012.source: You say they are coming here. Do they have ships?

RoA_A01_CNMC1_VO_S003_013.source: I do not know, but even if they did not have ships before, they certainly will have them soon — they raided all along our coastline and captured our wharfs. They can and will use them to build more ships.

RoA_A01_CNMC1_VO_S003_014.source: We can assume, then, that they have the means to cross the ocean.

RoA_A01_CNMC1_VO_S003_015.source: You are telling us to prepare for war then?!

RoA_A01_CNMC1_VO_S003_016.source: Yes, a war for the very survival of our race.

RoA_A01_CNMC1_VO_S004_001.source: We need to begin massive war preparations.

RoA_A01_CNMC1_VO_S004_002.source: While the kingdoms are assembling their armies, I will send a small scouting force to the island of Zul’Dare, to evaluate if the Horde has made any attempts to cross the Great Sea yet.

RoA_A01_CNMC1_VO_S004_003.source: Our shores have long stood vigil over this continent, and we’ve thrown all sorts of pirates back into the sea. I am certain we can do so again. I don’t see the necessity of unifying our armies into a single force.

RoA_A01_CNMC1_VO_S004_004.source: I admit that the situation is serious, but I need to consult with my lawmakers and ministers before I can subscribe to any kind of war. This is how things are done in civilized kingdoms.

RoA_A01_CNMC1_VO_S004_005.source: What bureaucratic nonsense, Aiden! If you can't take responsibility, then leave it to those who can.

RoA_A01_CNMC1_VO_S004_006.source: Your Majesties, we’ve told you, these are no mere raiders, this is death riding on dark tides! You will not be able to withstand them on your own, only together, all of us!

RoA_A01_CNMC1_VO_S004_007.source: On that I agree Sir Lothar. However, if we are to establish this union, I only see fit that I lead it to battle!

RoA_A01_CNMC1_VO_S004_008.source: Do I need to remind at whose behest are we here? Who sponsored this council from the beginning? We need more than a mere battle commander, we need someone who can unite us.

RoA_A01_CNMC1_VO_S004_009.source: So, you have come to claim sovereignty over us?

RoA_A01_CNMC1_VO_S004_010.source: Of course not. I am merely saying the command of our unified forces should not be decided lightly.

RoA_A01_CNMC1_VO_S004_011.source: The Kirin Tor agrees. This is not a matter of military might or political influence, we need a unifying presence. A presence experienced with this sort of threat.

RoA_A01_CNMC1_VO_S004_012.source: There’s wisdom to your words, Lord Antonidas. Kul Tiras owns the seas, and I am ready to lead our fleets into battle if need be, but to command…

RoA_A01_CNMC1_VO_S004_013.source: I see where you are going…If I may, great leaders. When Northshire was decimated by the foul magic of orcish warlocks, we despaired. And when King Llane fell and Stormwind burned, we lost all hope.

RoA_A01_CNMC1_VO_S004_014.source: Yet there was one man who rallied us and led us through darkness. Anduin Lothar.

RoA_A01_CNMC1_VO_S004_015.source: Indeed… I agree. As the champion of Azeroth, Lord Lothar spent years battling the orcish hordes. His experience alone is invaluable.

RoA_A01_CNMC1_VO_S004_016.source: And his bloodline. My ancestors fought beside Thoradin before, it would be a great honor to do so again by following his descendant.

RoA_A01_CNMC1_VO_S004_017.source: Kul Tiras agrees with this assessment. Sir Lothar is the perfect choice.

RoA_A01_CNMC1_VO_S004_018.source: A wise decision. The Kirin Tor stands behind you.

RoA_A01_CNMC1_VO_S004_019.source: If the majority agrees, so do I.

RoA_A01_CNMC1_VO_S004_020.source: Gilneas shall honor the pact, Lord Lothar. We will provide any troops, armor, weaponry, food we can spare.

RoA_A01_CNMC1_VO_S004_021.source: Now then, we have all agreed that time is of the essence if we are to marshal our united forces and meet the Horde when it arrives.

RoA_A01_CNMC1_VO_S004_022.source: Do we also agree upon our course of action?

RoA_A01_CNMC1_VO_S004_023.source: Then I hereby declare the founding of the Grand Alliance of Lordaeron!

RoA_A01_CNMC1_VO_S004_024.source: We shall stand together as one, as our ancestors did long ago, in the Arathi Empire. And it is only fitting, then, that our commander should hail from that ancient ruling stock.

RoA_A01_CNMC1_VO_S004_025.source: We the kings of the Alliance do hereby appoint Lord Anduin Lothar, Champion of Azeroth, as our Supreme Commander!

RoA_A01_CNMC1_VO_S004_026.source: This is great honor for me. I accept this charge. With your aid, noble kings, we will vanquish the Horde and spare your people from the gruesome fate of Stormwind!

RoA_A01_CNMC1_VO_S006_001.source: Three years of bloody war have devastated the Eastern Kingdoms. Ironforge is under siege, the forests of Quel'Thalas burn with dragonfire, and countless cities lie in ruins.

RoA_A01_CNMC1_VO_S006_002.source: Kul Tiran and Gilnean forces remain isolated in their coastal enclaves. The capitals of Lordaeron and Dalaran are besieged by the Horde.

RoA_A01_CNMC1_VO_S006_003.source: Meanwhile, Anduin Lothar gathers his forces in Andorhal, rallying after a fierce battle with orcish raiders…

RoA_A01_CNMC1_VO_S006_004.source: You seem discouraged, my lord… What happened?

RoA_A01_CNMC1_VO_S006_005.source: I just received a message that the Cross Island has fallen. It means that the Horde has reached Lordamere Lake. Seems like Alterac forces cannot defend their borders anymore… Since the beginning of this war, fortune has been against us.

RoA_A01_CNMC1_VO_S006_006.source: We have been chasing Doomhammer across the continent, never to arrive in time…

RoA_A01_CNMC1_VO_S006_007.source: Yet we managed to save Tyr's Hand. And Andorhal.

RoA_A01_CNMC1_VO_S006_008.source: Yes, but we have lost Stromgarde, Tol Barad, Stratholme and much, much more…

RoA_A01_CNMC1_VO_S006_009.source: But this is no time to ponder. Willhelm, travel north, reach Commander Turalyon. It’s imperative he reinforces the capital’s defenses as soon as possible.

RoA_A01_CNMC1_VO_S006_011.source: Danath, I need you to travel south with a small squad of your troops. Check the situation in Southshore.

RoA_A01_CNMC1_VO_S006_012.source: Why move south if the Horde is concentrated between the capital and Dalaran?

RoA_A01_CNMC1_VO_S006_013.source: We will secure Hillsbrad and cut orcish supply lines from the sea. Then we will hit Doomhammer’s army from the rear.

RoA_A01_CNMC1_VO_S006_014.source: This time, we will not play his game. Instead of chasing him, we’ll place a noose around the neck of his blasted Horde… and hang them with it.

RoA_A01_CNMC1_VO_S006_015.source: I admit that is a wise choice, but Lordaeron and Dalaran require our help right now.

RoA_A01_CNMC1_VO_S006_016.source: Trust me, Danath. I am already preparing necessary actions to aid and secure them.

RoA_A01_CNMC1_VO_S006_017.source: Understood, Commander. We will march to Southshore today.

RoA_A01_CNMC1_VO_S006_018.source: Gavinrad. You will travel south with Captain Danath and provide support to his forces. After that, you will remain there and oversee the region in case the Horde attempts to flee east through Alterac. If you spot them - show no mercy.

RoA_A01_CNMC1_VO_S006_019.source: I won’t fail you, Commander Lothar.
